page.title: Last Z Siege to Seize Costs (2026) — Total Badge Cost and Aggressive Path
page.meta_description: Siege to Seize badge costs in Last Z: total branch cost, aggressive research path, final Recharge Shield totals, and the exact node costs before Field Research unlocks.
page.keywords: Last Z Siege to Seize cost, Last Z Siege to Seize badges, Last Z Recharge Shield cost, Last Z Field Research unlock, Last Z aggressive research
page.h1: Last Z Siege to Seize Costs — Total Badge Cost and Aggressive Path
page.headline: Last Z Siege to Seize Costs — Total Badge Cost and Aggressive Path
page.guide_meta: Research Costs • Updated March 15, 2026 • Branch breakdown

guide_verified: Siege to Seize is the more aggressive combat branch after Military Strategies. It is useful, but it is usually not the first place most players should dump badges if Peace Shield value is still missing.
data_lede: Use this page to see the total Siege to Seize badge cost, understand the branch structure, and judge how much you need before the major late checkpoints and Field Research unlock path.

quick_answer.lede: Best Siege to Seize rule for most players: treat it as the aggressive follow-up branch after your core survival research is already in place. The full branch costs 267,375 badges and is usually stronger once Peace Shield essentials are done first.

items.title: Why it matters
items.detail: Aggressive path that leads toward Field Research

quick_answer.callout: Siege to Seize improves offensive pressure, but many accounts get better practical value by securing Peace Shield before they commit fully here.

summary.title: Siege to Seize Summary
summary.body: Siege to Seize is one of the core aggressive research branches in Last Z. It is a real branch, not a side branch, but it usually makes the most sense once your account already has the key survivability tools that Peace Shield provides.

tree.title: Siege to Seize Tree Overview
tree.lede: This overview groups the full Siege to Seize branch into node totals, so you can see the early aggressive path, the Demolition Crew checkpoints, and the final Recharge Shield route.

table.title: Full Siege to Seize Node Cost Table
table.intro: These totals show how many badges each Siege to Seize node consumes across its full level range. Use this view when you care about the branch cost before you commit to the aggressive path.

requirements.title: Unlock Requirements and Why This Branch Matters
requirements.intro: Siege to Seize opens before the deepest late-game branches, but it is still a serious badge commitment. It works best as a deliberate aggressive path, not as a random detour.
bullets: 100% Siege to Seize unlocks Field Research.
This branch is usually stronger after your key Peace Shield value is already secured.
requirements.followup: If you are using badges mainly for safer PvP value, Peace Shield often deserves priority before a full Siege to Seize push.

items.title: Early branch setup
items.text: The first six nodes get you through the early aggressive frame of the branch without yet touching the expensive late nodes.

items.title: Demolition Crew II reached
items.text: This is the first clearly expensive checkpoint and a good line for deciding whether you want to keep going on the aggressive path.

items.title: Late pre-final block
items.text: After the defensive trio, the branch turns into two very expensive 20-level nodes plus the final Recharge Shield.

items.title: Full branch
items.text: Finishing Siege to Seize is a serious badge investment and is often best justified when you are already planning Field Research next.

next_steps.title: What Comes Next After Siege to Seize
paragraphs: For many accounts, the main reason to fully finish Siege to Seize is not the branch by itself, but what it unlocks next: Field Research. If that is your plan, this page gives you the exact badge commitment before you cross that line.
If you are still deciding between aggressive and defensive badge allocation, compare this branch against Peace Shield Costs before committing fully.

faq.question: How many badges does Siege to Seize cost in Last Z?
faq.answer: Siege to Seize costs 267,375 badges in total. It is a large branch and should usually be treated as a deliberate mid-to-late combat investment.

faq.question: What unlocks Siege to Seize?
faq.answer: Siege to Seize unlocks after you reach 40% Military Strategies.

faq.question: Is Siege to Seize better than Peace Shield first?
faq.answer: Usually no. Most players get more immediate survivability and real combat value from Peace Shield before they fully commit to Siege to Seize.

faq.question: Does Siege to Seize unlock Field Research?
faq.answer: Yes. Reaching 100% Siege to Seize unlocks Field Research, which is one of the biggest reasons some accounts finish this branch.

faq.question: What is the key checkpoint in Siege to Seize?
faq.answer: A practical early checkpoint is Demolition Crew II at 58,295 cumulative badges, because it tells you whether you really want to continue toward the expensive late nodes.
